human-review.evidence: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18) approved after reviewing the full packet: all 7 autonomous gates green; 121 findings all closed or routed to the followups doc; full e2e 203/208 with the 5 failures proven environmental (OS clipboard lock episode, documented redirector contention, identical on pre-feature baseline); feature surface fully green including all phone-remote specs.

escalations.reason: Wire an actually-scheduled drain trigger (ws.send callback or polling timer) for socketDrained. Mount PhoneRemoteSettings in SettingsPanel.tsx and gate it with a mount-path test. Thread real workspace id/name and pane titles into phoneRemoteLivePanes or get sign-off on single-synthetic-workspace as accepted v1 gap. Size the phone terminal from inventory cols/rows on select and route typed input through key-bar latch for Ctrl+letter (Ctrl+C) support. Route server errors and phone-remote enable-failures through app-wide error paths. Invoke deps.onSpawn/deps.onResize on remote-pane branches. Resolve auth-relaunch contradiction in spec (HttpOnly cookie or service worker); distinguish auth vs transient reconnect failures with backoff/terminal states. Replace undocumented single-workspace inventory with real workspace grouping or explicit v1 scope record in docs/deferred.md. Add e2e tests for backpressure saturation, resync arrival, app-close liveness, and served-client DOM interactions. 15 blocking findings.
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): loop back and fix all 15 blocking findings; none accepted as gaps. (1) REQ-011 inventory: implement REAL workspace grouping — thread real workspace id/name + pane titles into the inventory and include remote-workspace panes in server-side grid observability; the single-synthetic-workspace stub is rejected. (2) FINDING-025 auth contradiction: amend the spec to an HttpOnly session cookie — the first token-authenticated request sets it; GET / passes with token OR valid cookie, 401 otherwise; Regenerate revokes cookies too (cookie bound to token generation). This is a spec amendment, so shift-left to spec and re-propagate. (3) e2e coverage: add the requested tests (backpressure saturation, resync arrival, app-close liveness, served-client DOM) through the tests phase NOW, not deferred. Also fix mechanically: real drain trigger for socketDrained (ws.send callback or timer — ws emits no 'drain' event), mount PhoneRemoteSettings in SettingsPanel with a mount-path test, size the phone terminal from inventory cols/rows on select, key-bar Ctrl latch composing with soft-keyboard input, route enable-failures + server errors through app-wide error paths, invoke deps.onSpawn/onResize on remote-pane branches.

escalations.reason: FINDING-001/015/029/030: Choose drain trigger implementation (ws.send callback vs socket.drain vs polling). FINDING-010/024: Approve mounting PhoneRemoteSettings in SettingsPanel. FINDING-011/017/027: Decide workspace-metadata threading or accept v1 scope reduction. FINDING-016/031: Approve sizing client terminal from inventory cols/rows on select. FINDING-025: Choose authenticated-relaunch path (HttpOnly cookie / unauthenticated shell / service worker). FINDING-033: Approve routing input through Ctrl latch. FINDING-034: Approve routing errors app-wide. FINDING-040: Approve invoking deps.onSpawn/onResize on remote branches.
escalations.decision: Duplicate of ESC-001 (identical 15 findings and questions). The human decision recorded on ESC-001 (Kevin, 2026-07-18T16:26Z) stands and is being executed now: shift-left loopback to spec, fix all 15 blocking findings, none accepted as gaps — HttpOnly session cookie auth amendment, real workspace-grouped inventory, e2e coverage added through the tests phase, plus the mechanical fixes enumerated on ESC-001.

escalations.reason: Should we fix the burst queue to hold-window-aware ordering (syncing mirror barrier with session hold-windows, marking stale for lost bytes in attach/resync) to restore exactly-once per REQ-009/REQ-017, or defer this to v2?
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): fix all 5 now (FINDING-058/060/063/070/071), none deferred. Implement mode: human-authorized single Claude implementer at the router-computed producer tier, overriding a potential A/B mandate for this focused fix round (budget-conscious; frozen suite + re-review still arbitrate) — to be recorded via record-implementer as an audited deviation.

escalations.reason: Should the Tailscale pairing path be fixed to accept external HTTPS origins (not just hostnames), enabling pairing through Tailscale Serve as documented? Should REQ-031's reachability requirement be enforced?
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): fix now. Accept a full external HTTPS origin for the Tailscale path and build the pairing URL from it (REQ-031 reachability enforced under every documented access story). Single top-tier Claude implementer, same audited router-override as ESC-003.

escalations.reason: Should the final pane output on exit be preserved when a client is stale/saturated? Should a final barrier snapshot be captured before mirror disposal to ensure REQ-017/REQ-026 exactly-once delivery?
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): fix now. Preserve final pane output at exit for stale/saturated clients — final barrier snapshot before mirror disposal (or equivalent reorder) so REQ-017/REQ-026 exactly-once holds at pane exit. Single top-tier Claude implementer, same audited router-override as ESC-003.

escalations.reason: Accept external ORIGIN model (scheme/port distinct from backend) for Tailscale pairing support; accept final-output preservation via buffer-replacing resync for exiting stale panes (FINDING-083); accept ordered replay operations preventing resize from overtaking output (FINDING-084); accept staged/transactional token regeneration with persistence-first (FINDING-085); accept staged/transactional settings mutations with persistence-first (FINDING-086); accept removing resurrection fallback for exited panes on late resize (FINDING-093)
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): FINDING-082/083 were already fixed in 50b240c — the re-files were stale (closed with mechanical evidence by the orchestrator). Fix the four genuinely-new blockers now (085 transactional regenerate, 093 exited-pane resize guard, 084 mirror resize write-barrier, 086 transactional settings lifecycle) via a single top-tier Claude implementer under the same audited router-override. This consumes the final implement loopback within the cap; the orchestrator verifies and closes the four with evidence before re-review round 5.

escalations.reason: Quick.json persistence race allows phone-remote config (enabled/bind/port/tokenHash) to be lost on app restart, invalidating all device pairings; decide whether to fix the write coordination or accept as v1 limitation
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): FIX FINDING-095 now — a settings feature erased by an ordinary renderer save or the quit flush is functionally broken, not a v1 limitation. Authorizes exceeding the implement iteration cap for this one round (cap override recorded here). Fix shape: single field owner — the main-side quick:save path preserves main-owned phoneRemote fields over any renderer whole-record payload; no tokenHash sent to the renderer. Single top-tier Claude implementer, same audited router-override.

escalations.reason: iteration cap exceeded (implement: 4/3, total: 12/10). ESC-007 resolved: FINDING-095 quick.json two-writer clobber — renderer whole-record saves erase main-owned phoneRemote fields on any settings save or quit flush. Human authorized cap override on ESC-007.
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): cap override AUTHORIZED for exactly one more implement round to fix FINDING-095 (quick.json two-writer clobber). Authorization originally recorded on ESC-007; this resolves the cap escalation itself. After this round: no further implement loopbacks — remaining non-blocking findings route to the followups doc.

escalations.reason: Should UI refresh the pairing URL after bind/port changes to reflect the current reachable endpoint? Should we broadcast token regeneration URL updates to all windows to prevent stale QRs? Should phoneRemoteSend prioritize renderer IPC over phone-remote listeners to preserve timing semantics? Should Tailscale CLI documentation be updated to use current syntax?
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): fix all four (108 pairing-URL re-fetch on bind/port change, 109 renderer-send-before-phone-listeners timing, 110 cross-window regenerate URL refresh, 113 Tailscale CLI syntax), single top-tier implementer under the standing audited override, with a final cap raise (implement 7, global 18). REVIEW CLOSE-OUT: after these are fixed, gated, and closed on evidence, the review gate derives from the findings ledger WITHOUT a seventh reviewer fan-out — six rounds have run against a configured review cap of 3; remaining non-blocking findings route to the per-feature followups doc; human-review remains the final veto.

escalations.reason: Three late-filed cross-check blockers (119/120/121) surfaced after the ESC-009 close-out snapshot; straggler jobs now stopped, ledger static. Fix vs accept needs the human call (threshold HIGH / contract flags).
escalations.decision: Human (Kevin, 2026-07-18): fix all three (121 spawn-failure ghost-entry ordering, 119 held-push latest-wins clear, 120 client exited-guard on reconnect) using the final in-cap implement loopback, single top-tier implementer under the standing audited override. Ledger is static (straggler Codex jobs stopped) — after these close, the review gate derives clean with certainty.

implementer.reason: ESC-010 final round (last in-cap loopback): single top-tier Claude implementer fixed the three static-ledger blockers; implement gate re-passed; findings closed on mechanical evidence. Straggler Codex cross-check jobs were stopped before this round (ledger static), per the ESC-009/010 close-out plan.

loopbacks.reason: Cross-feature frozen-pin conflict: TEST-741 (0016) hard-pins the register-*.ts registrar count at 19 and CAPABILITY_IDS as their closed union; 0026's frozen TEST-2686 mandates the new register-phone-remote.ts (count 20). TEST-741's own header documents the amendment convention for exactly this case; the 0026 tests-phase collision survey missed it. Amending the pin + CAPABILITY_IDS from ground truth.

ESC-001 human decision: FINDING-025 is a requirement contradiction originating in spec (REQ-005 401-on-token-less-GET vs PWA relaunch) — amend to the HttpOnly session-cookie model and re-propagate; REQ-011 must specify the REAL workspace-grouped inventory (synthetic-workspace stub rejected); e2e coverage for backpressure saturation, resync arrival, app-close liveness, served-client DOM added in the tests phase. Remaining 13 blocking findings are implement-phase fixes carried by the re-descent.

Frozen-test authoring defect (corroborated independently by BOTH A/B candidates): tests/main/phone-remote-service.test.ts's mkPaneSourceV2 ends its panes object with 'as never' (line 498), stripping contextual typing from the four un-annotated (cb) arrow params at 492-495 — npm run typecheck fails TS7006 on a file no implementation choice can affect. Behavior-neutral amendment: annotate the four params to match their callback arrays. npm test unaffected.

ESC-003 resolved: 5 new HIGH findings on round-2 code, all implement-origin — burst-queue macrotask defer breaks snapshot-stream exactly-once (058/060), startup failure invisible pre-window (063), Set-Cookie dead after desktop restart (070), failed start destroys enabled intent (071).

ESC-004/005 resolved: FINDING-082 (pairing URL bypasses tailscale-serve HTTPS proxy — externalHost must accept a full origin) and FINDING-083 (final pane output lost at exit under stale/saturation — mirror disposed before exit fan-out). Both implement-origin.

ESC-006 resolved: four new blocking findings on the round-3/4 code — non-transactional regenerate (085) and settings lifecycle (086), mirror resize without write barrier (084), pty:resize-after-exit pane resurrection (093). All implement-origin; final loopback within cap.
ESC-007/008: FINDING-095 quick.json two-writer clobber fix — human-authorized cap raise (implement 3→5, global 10→14) recorded on both escalations; final implement round.
ESC-009: four small blockers from review round 6 (pairing-URL staleness x2, REQ-015 timing inversion, doc syntax). Final round under the ESC-009-recorded cap raise and close-out plan.
ESC-010: final three static-ledger blockers (119/120/121). Last in-cap round.
